title: Foot sprain - aftercare
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Foot sprain aftercare can include rest, protection, ice, compression, elevation, and gradual return to activity as symptoms improve.
Clinician-directed splints, braces, or crutches may be part of early protection for foot sprain recovery.
Escalation is appropriate for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, color change, fever, or inability to walk.
Follow-up may be needed when pain, swelling, or walking ability is not improving as expected.
